metaTitle: API — Prosperity | Loot Modifier API for Mod Integration
metaDescription: Prosperity's Loot Modifier API for mod developers: a Fabric-style event that adjusts loot generation after distance scaling, plus datapack-driven loot injection.
ogDescription: Loot Modifier API and event for integrating with Prosperity.
title: Developers
lede: Prosperity exposes a stable, Fabric-style Loot Modifier API so other mods can adjust loot generation — without a hard dependency.

html: Compile against Prosperity with modCompileOnly so it stays optional at runtime, then guard every call with a mod-loaded check.
Guard calls so your mod loads cleanly whether or not Prosperity is present:

The API classes live in com.rfizzle.prosperity.api and are annotated @ApiStatus.Stable. LootModifierCallback.EVENT fires after distance scaling but before the loot table resolves, handing each listener a mutable LootModifierContext.

Datapacks can add custom items to existing vanilla loot tables, gated by a minimum distance tier. Files live at data/prosperity/loot_injections/<name>.json and use tier names local, frontier, wilderness, outlands, depths.

blocks.html: Stub — the full injection file schema and the LootModifierContext method reference will be documented here.

items: The API package is com.rfizzle.prosperity.api — everything outside it is internal and may change without notice.
LootModifierCallback is a standard Fabric Event, not a custom event bus.
LootModifierContext is created fresh per loot generation, populated with the player, position, loot table, and post-distance-scaling values.
Mob loot scaling fires the same event, so listeners registered for container loot affect mob loot too.

html: Building an integration and something's missing? Open an issue — the API surface grows based on real integration needs.
